1→---
     2→title: Architecture
     3→description: How Skill Forge works — output format, confidence model, progressive tiers, tool ecosystem, and key design decisions
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Architecture
     7→
     8→This page explains how SKF works under the hood — the output format, confidence model, progressive capability tiers, tool ecosystem, and the design decisions that make every instruction traceable.
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## Why Another Tool?
    13→
    14→AI agents hallucinate APIs. Not sometimes — constantly. The table below shows why every existing approach fails at scale:
    15→
    16→| Approach | Strength | Fatal Flaw |
    17→|----------|----------|------------|
    18→| `npx skills init` | Format compliant | Empty shell. 0% intelligence. |
    19→| LLM Summarization | High semantic context | Hallucination. Guesses parameters. No grounding. |
    20→| RAG / Context stuffing | Good retrieval | Fragmented. Finds snippets, fails to synthesize. |
    21→| Manual Authoring | High initial quality | Drift. Doesn't scale. |
    22→| Copilot/Cursor built-in | Convenient | Generic. Doesn't know YOUR integration patterns. |
    23→| **Skill Forge** | **Structural truth + automation** | **Rigid. (Feature, not bug.)** |
    24→
    25→SKF solves this by mechanically extracting function signatures, type definitions, and usage patterns from source code — then compiling them into verifiable, version-pinned skills that comply with the [agentskills.io specification](https://agentskills.io/specification).
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## Progressive Capability Model
    30→
    31→SKF uses an additive tier model. Each tier is the previous tier plus one tool. You never lose capability by adding a tool.
    32→
    33→| Tier | Tools | What You Get |
    34→|------|-------|-------------|
    35→| **Quick** | `gh_bridge` + `skill-check` + `tessl` | Source reading + spec validation + content quality review. Best-effort skills in under a minute. |
    36→| **Forge** | + `ast_bridge` | Structural truth. AST-verified signatures. Co-import detection. T1 confidence. |
    37→| **Deep** | + `qmd_bridge` | Knowledge search. Temporal provenance. Drift detection. Full intelligence. |
    38→
    39→Setup detects your installed tools and sets your tier automatically:
    40→
    41→```
    42→@Ferris SF
    43→```
    44→
    45→```
    46→Forge initialized. Tools: gh, ast-grep, QMD. Tier: Deep. Ready.
    47→```
    48→
    49→Don't have ast-grep or QMD yet? No problem — Quick mode works with just the GitHub CLI. Install tools later; your tier upgrades automatically.
    50→
    51→### Tier Override — Comparing Output Across Tiers
    52→
    53→You can force a specific tier by setting `tier_override` in your preferences file (`_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/preferences.yaml`):
    54→
    55→```yaml
    56→# Force Forge tier regardless of detected tools
    57→tier_override: Forge
    58→```
    59→
    60→This is useful for comparing skill quality across tiers for the same target:
    61→
    62→```
    63→# 1. Set tier_override: Quick in preferences.yaml
    64→@Ferris CS                # compile at Quick tier
    65→
    66→# 2. Change to tier_override: Forge
    67→@Ferris CS                # recompile at Forge tier — compare output
    68→
    69→# 3. Reset to tier_override: ~ (auto-detect)
    70→```
    71→
    72→Set `tier_override` to `Quick`, `Forge`, or `Deep`. Set to `~` (null) to return to auto-detection. The override is respected by all tier-aware workflows (CS, SS, US, AS, TS).
    73→
    74→---
    75→
    76→## Confidence Tiers
    77→
    78→Every claim in a generated skill carries a confidence tier that traces to its source:
    79→
    80→| Tier | Source | Tool | What It Means |
    81→|------|--------|------|---------------|
    82→| **T1** | AST extraction | `ast_bridge` | Current code, structurally verified. Immutable for that version. |
    83→| **T2** | QMD evidence / source reading | `qmd_bridge` / `gh_bridge` | Historical + planned context (issues, PRs, changelogs, docs). |
    84→| **T3** | External documentation | `doc_fetcher` | External, untrusted. Quarantined. |
    85→
    86→### Temporal Provenance
    87→
    88→Confidence tiers map to temporal scopes:
    89→
    90→- **T1-now (instructions):** What ast-grep sees in the checked-out code. This is what your agent executes.
    91→- **T2-past (annotations):** Closed issues, merged PRs, changelogs — why the API looks the way it does.
    92→- **T2-future (annotations):** Open PRs, deprecation warnings, RFCs — what's coming.
    93→
    94→Progressive disclosure controls how much context surfaces at each level:
    95→
    96→| Output | Content |
    97→|--------|---------|
    98→| `context-snippet.md` | T1-now only — compressed, always-on |
    99→| `SKILL.md` | T1-now + lightweight T2 annotations |
   100→| `references/` | Full temporal context with all tiers |
   101→
   102→### Tier Constrains Authority
   103→
   104→Your forge tier limits what authority claims a skill can make:
   105→
   106→| Forge Tier | AST? | QMD? | Max Authority | Accuracy Guarantee |
   107→|-----------|------|------|---------------|-------------------|
   108→| Quick | No | No | `community` | Best-effort |
   109→| Forge | Yes | No | `official` | Structural (AST-verified) |
   110→| Deep | Yes | Yes | `official` | Full (structural + contextual + temporal) |
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→## Output Architecture
   115→
   116→### Per-Skill Output
   117→
   118→Every generated skill produces a self-contained directory:
   119→
   120→```
   121→skills/{name}/
   122→├── SKILL.md              # Active skill (loaded on trigger)
   123→├── context-snippet.md    # Passive context (compressed, always-on)
   124→├── metadata.json         # Machine-readable provenance
   125→└── references/           # Progressive disclosure
   126→    ├── {function-a}.md
   127→    ├── {function-b}.md
   128→    └── integrations/     # Stack skills only
   129→        ├── auth-db.md
   130→        └── pwa-auth.md
   131→```
   132→
   133→### SKILL.md Format
   134→
   135→Skills follow the [agentskills.io specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) with frontmatter:
   136→
   137→```yaml
   138→---
   139→name: payment-service
   140→version: 2.1.0
   141→description: Payment processing API skill — 23 verified functions
   142→author: org/payment-team
   143→---
   144→```
   145→
   146→Every instruction in the body traces to source:
   147→
   148→```
   149→Extracted: `getToken(userId: string, options?: TokenOptions): Promise<AuthToken>`
   150→[AST:src/auth/index.ts:L42]. Confidence: T1.
   151→```
   152→
   153→### metadata.json — The Birth Certificate
   154→
   155→Machine-readable provenance for every skill:
   156→
   157→```json
   158→{
   159→  "name": "payment-service",
   160→  "version": "2.1.0",
   161→  "skill_type": "individual",
   162→  "source_authority": "official",
   163→  "source_repo": "github.com/org/payment-service",
   164→  "source_commit": "a1b2c3d",
   165→  "forge_tier": "forge",
   166→  "spec_version": "1.3",
   167→  "generated_at": "2026-02-25T14:30:00Z",
   168→  "stats": {
   169→    "exports_documented": 23,
   170→    "exports_total": 23,
   171→    "coverage": 1.0,
   172→    "confidence_t1": 20,
   173→    "confidence_t2": 3,
   174→    "confidence_t3": 0
   175→  }
   176→}
   177→```
   178→
   179→### Stack Skill Output
   180→
   181→Stack skills map how your dependencies interact — shared types, co-import patterns, integration points:
   182→
   183→```
   184→skills/{project}-stack/
   185→├── SKILL.md              # Integration patterns + project conventions
   186→├── context-snippet.md    # Compressed stack index
   187→├── metadata.json         # Component versions, integration graph
   188→└── references/
   189→    ├── nextjs.md         # Project-specific subset
   190→    ├── better-auth.md    # Project-specific subset
   191→    └── integrations/
   192→        ├── auth-db.md    # Cross-library pattern
   193→        └── pwa-auth.md   # Cross-library pattern
   194→```
   195→
   196→The primary source is your project repo. Component references trace to library repos. `skill_type: "stack"` in metadata.
   197→
   198→---
   199→
   200→## Dual-Output Strategy
   201→
   202→Based on [Vercel research](https://vercel.com/blog/agents-md-outperforms-skills-in-our-agent-evals): passive context (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) achieves 100% pass rate vs 53% for active skills alone.
   203→
   204→Every skill generates both:
   205→
   206→1. **SKILL.md** — Active skill loaded on trigger with full instructions
   207→2. **context-snippet.md** — Passive context, compressed index injected into CLAUDE.md
   208→
   209→### Managed CLAUDE.md Section
   210→
   211→Export injects a managed section between markers:
   212→
   213→```markdown
   214→<!-- SKF:BEGIN updated:2026-02-25 -->
   215→[SKF Skills]|3 skills|1 stack
   216→|IMPORTANT: Prefer documented APIs over training data.
   217→|When using a listed library, read its SKILL.md before writing code.
   218→|
   219→|payment-service → skills/payment-service/
   220→|  Payment processing API — token management, session handling, webhooks
   221→|  exports: getToken, refreshToken, revokeSession, createSession
   222→|  refs: api-reference, type-definitions, webhook-patterns
   223→|
   224→|auth-service → skills/auth-service/
   225→|  Authentication service — session validation, user management, RBAC
   226→|  exports: getSession, validateToken, revokeSession, createUser
   227→|
   228→|my-project-stack → skills/my-project-stack/
   229→|  Full-stack integration patterns for Next.js + auth + DB + PWA
   230→|  stack: next@15, better-auth@3, spacetimedb@1, serwist@9
   231→|  integrations: auth↔db, pwa↔auth, gateway↔auth
   232→<!-- SKF:END -->
   233→```
   234→
   235→~50-80 tokens per skill (description + exports + refs). Developer controls placement. Ferris controls content. Snippet updates only happen at `export-skill` — create and update are draft operations.
   236→
   237→---
   238→
   239→## Tool Ecosystem
   240→
   241→### 6 Tools
   242→
   243→| Tool | Wraps | Purpose |
   244→|------|-------|---------|
   245→| **`gh_bridge`** | GitHub CLI (`gh`) | Source code access, issue mining, release tracking, PR intelligence |
   246→| **`skill-check`** | [thedaviddias/skill-check](https://github.com/thedaviddias/skill-check) | Validation + auto-fix (`check --fix`), quality scoring (0-100), security scan, split-body, diff comparison |
   247→| **`tessl`** | [tessl](https://tessl.io) | Content quality review, actionability scoring, progressive disclosure evaluation, AI judge with suggestions |
   248→| **`ast_bridge`** | ast-grep CLI | Structural extraction, custom AST queries, co-import detection |
   249→| **`qmd_bridge`** | QMD (local search) | BM25 keyword search, vector semantic search, collection indexing |
   250→| **`doc_fetcher`** | Environment web tools | Remote documentation fetching for T3-confidence content. Tool-agnostic — uses whatever web fetching is available (Firecrawl, WebFetch, curl, etc.). Output quarantined as T3. |
   251→
   252→### Conflict Resolution
   253→
   254→When tools disagree, higher priority wins for instructions. Lower priority is preserved as annotations:
   255→
   256→| Priority | Source | Tool |
   257→|----------|--------|------|
   258→| 1 (highest) | AST extraction | `ast_bridge` |
   259→| 2 | QMD evidence | `qmd_bridge` |
   260→| 3 | Source reading (non-AST) | `gh_bridge` |
   261→| 4 | External documentation | `doc_fetcher` |
   262→
   263→### Internal Utility
   264→
   265→**`manifest_reader`** detects and parses dependency files across ecosystems:
   266→
   267→- **Full support:** `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, `Cargo.toml`, `go.mod`
   268→- **Basic support:** `build.gradle`, `pom.xml`, `Gemfile`, `composer.json`
   269→
   270→---
   271→
   272→## Workspace Artifacts
   273→
   274→Build artifacts are committable — another developer can reproduce the same skill:
   275→
   276→```
   277→forge-data/{skill-name}/
   278→├── skill-brief.yaml        # Compilation config
   279→├── provenance-map.json     # Source map with AST bindings
   280→├── evidence-report.md      # Build audit trail
   281→└── extraction-rules.yaml   # Language-specific ast-grep schema
   282→```
   283→
   284→`skills/` and `forge-data/` are committed. Agent memory (`_bmad/_memory/forger-sidecar/`) is gitignored.
   285→
   286→---
   287→
   288→## Ownership Model
   289→
   290→| Context | `source_authority` | Distribution |
   291→|---------|-------------------|-------------|
   292→| OSS library (maintainer generates) | `official` | `npx skills publish` to agentskills ecosystem |
   293→| Internal service (team generates) | `internal` | `skills/` in repo, ships with code |
   294→| External dependency (consumer generates) | `community` | Local `skills/`, marked as community |
   295→
   296→Provenance maps enable verification: an `official` skill's provenance must trace to the actual source repo owned by the author.
   297→
   298→---
   299→
   300→## Key Design Decisions
   301→
   302→| Decision | Rationale |
   303→|----------|-----------|
   304→| **Solo agent (Ferris), not multi-agent** | One domain (skill compilation) doesn't benefit from handoffs. Shared knowledge base (AST patterns, provenance maps) is the core asset. |
   305→| **Workflows drive modes, not conversation** | Ferris doesn't auto-switch based on question content. Invoke a workflow to change mode. Predictable behavior. |
   306→| **Hub-and-spoke cross-knowledge** | Each skill has one primary source. Cross-repo references use inline summary + pointer + `[XREF:repo:file:line]` provenance tag. |
   307→| **Stack skill = compositional** | SKILL.md is the integration layer. references/ contains per-library + integration pairs. Partial regeneration on dependency updates. |
   308→| **Snippet updates only at export** | Create/update are draft operations. Export publishes to skills/ and CLAUDE.md. No half-baked snippets. |
   309→| **Bundle spec with opt-in update** | Offline-capable. 90-day staleness warning. `setup-forge --update-spec` fetches latest. |
   310→
   311→---
   312→
   313→## Security
   314→
   315→- All tool wrappers use array-style subprocess execution — no shell interpolation
   316→- Input sanitization: allowlist characters for repo names, file paths, patterns
   317→- File paths validated against project root (no directory traversal)
   318→- **Source code never leaves the machine.** All processing is local (AST, QMD, validation).
   319→- `doc_fetcher` informs users which URLs will be fetched externally before processing
   320→
   321→---
   322→
   323→## Ecosystem Alignment
   324→
   325→SKF produces skills compatible with the [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io) ecosystem:
   326→
   327→- Full [specification](https://agentskills.io/specification) compliance
   328→- Distribution via [`npx skills add/publish`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/skills)
   329→- Compatible with [agentskills/agentskills](https://github.com/agentskills/agentskills) and [vercel-labs/skills](https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills)
   330→

1→---
     2→name: 'step-03-re-extract'
     3→description: 'Tier-aware AST extraction on changed files only, producing fresh exports with confidence tiers'
     4→
     5→nextStepFile: './step-04-merge.md'
     6→---
     7→
     8→# Step 3: Re-Extract Changed Exports
     9→
    10→## STEP GOAL:
    11→
    12→Perform tier-aware extraction on only the changed files identified in step 02, producing fresh export data with confidence tier labels (T1/T1-low/T2) that will be merged into the existing skill in step 04.
    13→
    14→## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15→
    16→### Universal Rules:
    17→
    18→- 🛑 NEVER generate content without user input
    19→- 📖 CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    20→- 🔄 CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    21→- 📋 YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    22→- ⚙️ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    23→- ✅ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    24→
    25→### Role Reinforcement:
    26→
    27→- ✅ You are a precision code analyst operating in Surgeon mode
    28→- ✅ Zero-hallucination principle: every extracted instruction must include AST file:line citation
    29→- ✅ Confidence tier labels are MANDATORY on all extracted content
    30→- ✅ You bring AST-backed structural analysis; the source code provides ground truth
    31→
    32→### Step-Specific Rules:
    33→
    34→- 🎯 Focus ONLY on extracting changed exports — do not merge or modify existing skill
    35→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to extract unchanged files — only files in the change manifest
    36→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to modify SKILL.md — extraction produces intermediate data for step 04
    37→- 💬 DO NOT BE LAZY — For EACH changed file, launch a subprocess for deep AST analysis (Pattern 2)
    38→- ⚙️ If subprocess unavailable, perform extraction sequentially in main thread
    39→
    40→## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    41→
    42→- 🎯 Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    43→- 💾 Produce structured extraction results per changed file
    44→- 📖 Label every extraction with confidence tier
    45→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to hallucinate exports — only extract what exists in source
    46→
    47→## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    48→
    49→- Available: change manifest from step 02, forge tier from step 01, source files
    50→- Focus: extraction only — no merge, no write
    51→- Limits: only changed files from manifest
    52→- Dependencies: step 02 must have produced change manifest
    53→
    54→## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55→
    56→**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    57→
    58→### 1. Check for Docs-Only Mode
    59→
    60→**If `source_type: "docs-only"` in the original brief or metadata:**
    61→
    62→"**Docs-only skill detected.** This skill was generated from external documentation, not source code. Re-extraction will re-fetch the original `doc_urls` to check for updated content."
    63→
    64→- Re-fetch each URL from `doc_urls` (from the brief or metadata) using whatever web fetching capability is available
    65→- Extract updated API information with T3 `[EXT:{url}]` citations
    66→- Build the updated extraction inventory from fetched content
    67→- Skip all source code extraction below — proceed directly to the merge step (section 5 or equivalent)
    68→
    69→**If `source_type: "source"` (default):** Continue with source extraction below.
    70→
    71→### 1b. Determine Extraction Strategy by Tier
    72→
    73→**Remote Source Resolution (Forge/Deep only):**
    74→
    75→If `source_root` is a local path: proceed with the tier-appropriate strategy as normal.
    76→
    77→If `source_root` (from metadata.json) is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) AND tier is Forge or Deep:
    78→
    79→1. **Check `git` availability:** Verify `git` is functional (`git --version`). If `git` is not available, skip to the fallback warning below.
    80→
    81→2. **Ephemeral sparse clone:** Clone only the changed files from the change manifest to a system temp path. Note: at this point in the flow, `{source_root}` is known to be a remote URL (the local-path case was already handled above).
    82→
    83→   ```
    84→   temp_path = {system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/
    85→   git clone --depth 1 --single-branch --filter=blob:none --sparse {source_root} {temp_path}
    86→   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set {changed_files_from_manifest}
    87→   ```
    88→
    89→   No `--branch` flag is used — the clone targets the remote's default branch, which must match the branch used during the original [CS] Create Skill run. This scopes the clone to only the files identified in step-02's change manifest, avoiding a full repository download.
    90→
    91→3. **If clone succeeds:** Update the working source path to `{temp_path}` for all subsequent AST operations in this step. Proceed with the **Forge tier** extraction strategy below. Mark `ephemeral_clone_active = true` for cleanup.
    92→
    93→4. **If clone fails (network error, auth failure, timeout):**
    94→
    95→   ⚠️ **Warn the user explicitly:**
    96→
    97→   "Ephemeral clone of `{source_root}` failed: {error}. Degrading to source reading (T1-low) for this run. For T1 (AST-verified) confidence, clone the repository locally and re-run [CS] Create Skill with the local path, then re-run this update."
    98→
    99→   Override the extraction strategy to Quick tier for this run. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   100→
   101→**Ephemeral clone cleanup:** After extraction is complete for all files in scope (whether successful or partially failed), before presenting the extraction summary (Section 5), if `ephemeral_clone_active`, delete the `{temp_path}` directory. Log: "Ephemeral source clone cleaned up." This ensures cleanup runs even if some extractions failed, as long as the step itself is still executing.
   102→
   103→**If AST tool is unavailable at Forge/Deep tier (local source):**
   104→
   105→⚠️ **Warn the user explicitly:** "AST tools are unavailable — extraction will use source reading (T1-low). Run [SF] Setup Forge to detect and configure AST tools for T1 confidence."
   106→
   107→Degrade to Quick tier extraction. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   108→
   109→**Quick tier (text pattern matching):**
   110→- Extract function/class/type names via regex patterns
   111→- Extract export statements via text matching
   112→- Confidence: T1-low (pattern-matched, not AST-verified)
   113→
   114→**Forge tier (AST structural extraction):**
   115→
   116→⚠️ **CRITICAL:** Follow the **AST Extraction Protocol** from `src/workflows/create-skill/data/extraction-patterns.md`. Use the decision tree based on the number of changed files: prefer MCP `find_code()` for small sets, `find_code_by_rule()` with scoped YAML rules for medium sets, and CLI `--json=stream` with line-by-line streaming for large sets. Never use `ast-grep --json` (without `=stream`) — it loads the entire result set into memory and will fail on large codebases.
   117→
   118→- Extract: function signatures, type definitions, class members, exported constants
   119→- Extract: parameter types, return types, JSDoc/docstring comments
   120→- Confidence: T1 (AST-verified structural truth)
   121→
   122→**Deep tier (AST + QMD semantic enrichment):**
   123→- Perform all Forge tier extractions (T1)
   124→- Additionally: launch a subprocess that queries qmd_bridge for temporal context on changed exports, returning T2 evidence per export
   125→- QMD provides: usage patterns, historical context, related documentation
   126→- Confidence: T1 for structural, T2 for semantic enrichment
   127→
   128→### 2. Extract Changed Files
   129→
   130→DO NOT BE LAZY — For EACH file in the change manifest with status MODIFIED, ADDED, or RENAMED, launch a subprocess that:
   131→
   132→1. Loads the source file
   133→2. Performs tier-appropriate extraction (Quick/Forge/Deep)
   134→3. For each export found:
   135→   - Record: export name, type (function/class/type/constant), signature
   136→   - Record: file path, start line, end line
   137→   - Record: parameters with types (if function/method)
   138→   - Record: return type (if function/method)
   139→   - Record: JSDoc/docstring summary (if present)
   140→   - Label: confidence tier (T1/T1-low/T2)
   141→4. Returns structured extraction findings to parent
   142→
   143→**For DELETED files:** No extraction needed — deletions handled in merge step.
   144→
   145→**For MOVED files:** Re-extract at new location to update file:line references.
   146→
   147→### 3. Deep Tier QMD Enrichment (Conditional)
   148→
   149→**ONLY if forge_tier == Deep:**
   150→
   151→Read the `qmd_collections` registry from `{sidecar_path}/forge-tier.yaml`.
   152→
   153→Find the collection entry matching the current skill: look for an entry where `skill_name` matches the skill being updated AND `type` is `"extraction"`.
   154→
   155→**If a matching extraction collection is found:**
   156→Launch a subprocess that loads qmd_bridge and for each changed export:
   157→1. Queries the `{skill_name}-extraction` collection for semantic context related to the export
   158→2. Searches for usage patterns, documentation references, temporal history
   159→3. Returns T2 evidence per export (usage frequency, context snippets, related concepts)
   160→
   161→**If no matching collection found in registry:**
   162→Log: "No QMD extraction collection found for {skill_name}. T2 enrichment skipped. Re-run [CS] Create Skill to generate the collection."
   163→Continue without T2 enrichment — extraction still produces T1 results.
   164→
   165→**If forge_tier != Deep:** Skip this section with notice: "QMD enrichment skipped (tier: {forge_tier})"
   166→
   167→### 4. Compile Extraction Results
   168→
   169→Aggregate all subprocess results into structured extraction data:
   170→
   171→```
   172→Extraction Results:
   173→  files_extracted: [count]
   174→  exports_extracted: [count]
   175→  confidence_breakdown:
   176→    T1: [count]
   177→    T1-low: [count]
   178→    T2: [count]
   179→
   180→  Per-file extractions:
   181→    {file_path}:
   182→      exports:
   183→        - name: {export_name}
   184→          type: function|class|type|constant
   185→          signature: {full signature}
   186→          location: {file}:{start_line}-{end_line}
   187→          confidence: T1|T1-low|T2
   188→          parameters: [{name, type}]
   189→          return_type: {type}
   190→          docstring: {summary}
   191→          qmd_evidence: {if Deep tier}
   192→```
   193→
   194→### 5. Display Extraction Summary and Auto-Proceed
   195→
   196→"**Re-Extraction Complete:**
   197→
   198→| Metric | Count |
   199→|--------|-------|
   200→| Files extracted | {count} |
   201→| Exports extracted | {count} |
   202→| T1 (AST-verified) | {count} |
   203→| T1-low (pattern-matched) | {count} |
   204→| T2 (QMD-enriched) | {count} |
   205→
   206→**Proceeding to merge with existing skill...**"
   207→
   208→### 6. Present MENU OPTIONS
   209→
   210→Display: "**Proceeding to merge...**"
   211→
   212→#### Menu Handling Logic:
   213→
   214→- After extraction results are compiled, immediately load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   215→
   216→#### EXECUTION RULES:
   217→
   218→- This is an auto-proceed step with no user choices
   219→- Proceed directly to next step after extraction completes
   220→
   221→## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   222→
   223→ONLY WHEN all changed files have been extracted and results compiled will you load {nextStepFile} to begin the merge operation.
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→## 🚨 SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   228→
   229→### ✅ SUCCESS:
   230→
   231→- Every changed file from manifest extracted with tier-appropriate method
   232→- All exports labeled with confidence tier (T1/T1-low/T2)
   233→- AST file:line citations on every extracted export
   234→- QMD enrichment performed for Deep tier (skipped with notice for Quick/Forge)
   235→- Extraction results compiled with per-file detail
   236→- Summary displayed before auto-proceeding
   237→- No unchanged files extracted
   238→
   239→### ❌ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   240→
   241→- Extracting unchanged files (wasting resources)
   242→- Missing confidence tier labels on any export
   243→- Hallucinating exports not present in source code
   244→- Skipping QMD enrichment at Deep tier
   245→- Modifying SKILL.md or any existing artifacts during extraction

1→# Extraction Patterns by Tier
     2→
     3→## Quick Tier (No AST)
     4→
     5→Source reading via gh_bridge — infer exports from file structure and content.
     6→
     7→### Strategy
     8→1. `gh_bridge.list_tree(owner, repo, branch)` — map source structure
     9→2. Identify entry points: index files, main exports, public modules
    10→3. `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, path)` — read each entry point
    11→4. Extract: exported function names, parameter lists, return types (from signatures)
    12→5. Infer types from JSDoc, docstrings, type annotations in source
    13→
    14→### Confidence
    15→- All results: T1-low (source reading without structural verification)
    16→- No co-import detection available
    17→- No AST-backed line numbers
    18→
    19→### Supported Patterns
    20→- `export function name(...)` / `export const name = ...` (JS/TS)
    21→- `pub fn name(...)` (Rust)
    22→- `def name(...)` with `__all__` (Python)
    23→- `func Name(...)` (Go, capitalized = exported)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Forge Tier (AST Available)
    28→
    29→Structural extraction via ast_bridge — verified exports with line-level citations.
    30→
    31→### Strategy
    32→1. Detect language from brief or file extensions
    33→2. `ast_bridge.scan_definitions(path, language)` — extract all exports
    34→3. For each export: function name, full signature, parameter types, return type, line number
    35→4. `ast_bridge.detect_co_imports(path, libraries[])` — find integration points
    36→5. Build extraction rules YAML for reproducibility
    37→
    38→### Confidence
    39→- Exported functions with full signatures: T1 (AST-verified)
    40→- Type definitions and interfaces: T1
    41→- Co-import patterns: T1
    42→- Internal/private functions: excluded (not part of public API)
    43→
    44→### ast-grep Patterns
    45→- JS/TS: `export function $NAME($$$PARAMS): $RET` / `export const $NAME`
    46→- Rust: `pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS) -> $RET`
    47→- Python: function definitions within `__all__` list
    48→- Go: capitalized function definitions
    49→
    50→---
    51→
    52→## Deep Tier (AST + QMD)
    53→
    54→Same extraction as Forge tier. Deep tier adds enrichment in step-04, not extraction.
    55→
    56→### Strategy
    57→- Identical to Forge tier extraction
    58→- QMD enrichment happens in the next step (step-04-enrich)
    59→- Extraction results carry forward unchanged
    60→
    61→### Confidence
    62→- Extraction: same as Forge (T1)
    63→- Enrichment annotations added in step-04: T2
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## AST Extraction Protocol
    68→
    69→When AST tools are available (Forge/Deep tier), follow this deterministic protocol to prevent output overflow on large codebases. The file count from step-01's file tree determines the extraction strategy.
    70→
    71→### Decision Tree
    72→
    73→Apply the first matching condition:
    74→
    75→```
    76→Files in scope ≤ 100
    77→  → Use ast-grep MCP tool: find_code(pattern, max_results=100, output_format="text")
    78→  → Parse compact text output directly into extraction inventory
    79→
    80→Files in scope 101–500
    81→  → Use ast-grep MCP tool: find_code_by_rule(yaml, max_results=150, output_format="text")
    82→  → Use scoped YAML rules (see recipes below) to filter at the AST level
    83→  → Parse compact text output into extraction inventory
    84→
    85→Files in scope > 500
    86→  → CLI streaming fallback: ast-grep --json=stream + line-by-line Python processing
    87→  → Process in directory batches, cap per-batch output
    88→  → Merge batch results into extraction inventory
    89→```
    90→
    91→### Safety Valve
    92→
    93→If any ast-grep operation (MCP or CLI) visibly causes a timeout, returns an error related to output size, or produces unexpectedly large output: immediately switch to the CLI streaming fallback with `--json=stream`. Do not retry the same approach. Note: `max_results` in the MCP tool and `| head -N` in the CLI path provide hard caps, but this safety valve covers cases where the upstream tool itself fails before returning results (e.g., OOM during JSON serialization).
    94→
    95→### MCP Tool Usage (Preferred)
    96→
    97→**Simple pattern search:**
    98→
    99→```
   100→find_code(
   101→  project_folder="{source_path}",
   102→  pattern="async def $NAME($$$PARAMS)",
   103→  language="python",
   104→  max_results=100,
   105→  output_format="text"
   106→)
   107→```
   108→
   109→**Scoped YAML rule search (for larger repos):**
   110→
   111→```
   112→find_code_by_rule(
   113→  project_folder="{source_path}",
   114→  yaml="id: public-api\nlanguage: python\nrule:\n  pattern: 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)'\n  inside:\n    kind: module\n    stopBy: end\nconstraints:\n  NAME:\n    regex: '^[^_]'",
   115→  max_results=150,
   116→  output_format="text"
   117→)
   118→```
   119→
   120→### CLI Streaming Fallback
   121→
   122→When MCP tools are unavailable or the repo exceeds 500 files in scope, use `--json=stream` (NEVER `--json` or `--json=pretty`) with line-by-line Python processing:
   123→
   124→```bash
   125→# Note: use $$$ for variadic params in ast-grep patterns (e.g., 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)')
   126→ast-grep -p '{pattern}' -l {language} --json=stream {path} | python3 -c "
   127→import sys, json
   128→for line in sys.stdin:
   129→    try:
   130→        m = json.loads(line)
   131→        v = m.get('metaVariables',{})
   132→        name = v.get('single',{}).get('NAME',{}).get('text','')
   133→        if name and not name.startswith('_'):
   134→            f = m.get('file','')
   135→            ln = m.get('range',{}).get('start',{}).get('line',0)+1
   136→            sig = m.get('text','').split(chr(10))[0].strip()
   137→            print(f'[AST:{f}:L{ln}] {sig}')
   138→    except: pass
   139→" | head -200
   140→```
   141→
   142→**Critical constraints:**
   143→
   144→- ALWAYS use `--json=stream` — never `--json` (loads entire array into memory)
   145→- ALWAYS process line-by-line (`for line in sys.stdin`) — never `json.load(sys.stdin)`
   146→- ALWAYS cap output with `| head -N` as a safety valve
   147→- For repos > 500 files, process in directory batches of 20-50 files each
   148→
   149→### YAML Rule Recipes by Language
   150→
   151→**Python — public functions:**
   152→
   153→```yaml
   154→id: python-public-functions
   155→language: python
   156→rule:
   157→  pattern: 'def $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   158→  inside:
   159→    kind: module
   160→    stopBy: end
   161→constraints:
   162→  NAME:
   163→    regex: '^[^_]'
   164→```
   165→
   166→**Python — public classes:**
   167→
   168→```yaml
   169→id: python-public-classes
   170→language: python
   171→rule:
   172→  pattern: 'class $NAME($$$BASES)'
   173→  inside:
   174→    kind: module
   175→    stopBy: end
   176→constraints:
   177→  NAME:
   178→    regex: '^[^_]'
   179→```
   180→
   181→**JavaScript/TypeScript — exported functions:**
   182→
   183→```yaml
   184→id: js-exported-functions
   185→language: typescript
   186→rule:
   187→  pattern: 'export function $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   188→```
   189→
   190→**JavaScript/TypeScript — exported constants:**
   191→
   192→```yaml
   193→id: js-exported-constants
   194→language: typescript
   195→rule:
   196→  pattern: 'export const $NAME = $VALUE'
   197→```
   198→
   199→**Rust — public functions:**
   200→
   201→```yaml
   202→id: rust-public-functions
   203→language: rust
   204→rule:
   205→  any:
   206→    - pattern: 'pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS) -> $RET'
   207→    - pattern: 'pub fn $NAME($$$PARAMS)'
   208→```
   209→
   210→**Go — exported functions (capitalized):**
   211→
   212→```yaml
   213→id: go-exported-functions
   214→language: go
   215→rule:
   216→  pattern: 'func $NAME($$$PARAMS) $RET'
   217→constraints:
   218→  NAME:
   219→    regex: '^[A-Z]'
   220→```
   221→
   222→---
   223→
   224→## Tier Degradation Rules
   225→
   226→### Remote Source at Forge/Deep Tier
   227→
   228→When `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) and the tier is Forge or Deep:
   229→
   230→- **ast-grep requires local files** — it cannot operate on remote URLs
   231→
   232→**Ephemeral clone strategy (preferred):**
   233→
   234→1. Check `git` availability (`git --version`). `git` is effectively guaranteed at Deep tier (via `gh` dependency) but NOT guaranteed at Forge tier.
   235→2. If `git` is available: perform an ephemeral shallow clone to a system temp path (`{system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/`).
   236→3. For create-skill: use `--depth 1 --single-branch --filter=blob:none`; apply sparse-checkout if `include_patterns` are specified.
   237→4. For update-skill: use sparse-checkout scoped to the changed files from the change manifest only. No `--branch` flag — uses the remote default branch (must match the branch used during original create-skill run).
   238→5. If clone succeeds: use the local clone path for AST extraction. All results are T1 with `[AST:...]` citations.
   239→6. Cleanup: delete the temp directory after extraction inventory is built and all data is in context. The clone never persists beyond the extraction step.
   240→
   241→**Fallback (clone fails or `git` unavailable):**
   242→
   243→- The extraction step MUST warn the user explicitly before degrading
   244→- **create-skill:** Warning must include actionable guidance — clone locally and update `source_repo` in the brief to the local path
   245→- **update-skill:** Warning must include actionable guidance — clone locally, re-run [CS] Create Skill with the local path to regenerate provenance data, then re-run the update
   246→- Extraction proceeds using Quick tier strategy (source reading via gh_bridge)
   247→- All results labeled T1-low with `[SRC:...]` citations
   248→- The degradation reason is recorded in the evidence report
   249→
   250→Silent degradation is **forbidden**. The user must always know when AST extraction was skipped and why.
   251→
   252→### AST Tool Unavailable at Forge/Deep Tier
   253→
   254→When the tier is Forge or Deep but ast-grep is not functional:
   255→
   256→- The extraction step MUST warn the user explicitly before degrading
   257→- Warning must include actionable guidance: run [SF] Setup Forge to detect tools
   258→- Extraction proceeds using Quick tier strategy
   259→- All results labeled T1-low
   260→- The degradation reason is recorded in the evidence report
   261→
   262→### Per-File AST Failure
   263→
   264→When ast-grep fails on an individual file (parse error, unsupported syntax):
   265→
   266→- Fall back to source reading for **that file only**
   267→- Other files continue with AST extraction
   268→- The affected file's results are labeled T1-low; unaffected files retain T1
   269→- Log a warning noting which file degraded and why
   270→

1→---
     2→title: Workflows
     3→description: All 10 SKF workflows with commands, steps, and connection diagram
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Workflows Reference
     7→
     8→SKF includes 10 workflows organized by purpose:
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## Core Workflows
    13→
    14→### Setup Forge (SF)
    15→
    16→**Command:** `@Ferris SF`
    17→
    18→**Purpose:** Initialize forge environment, detect tools, set capability tier, verify QMD collection health.
    19→
    20→**When to Use:** First time using SKF in a project. Run once per project.
    21→
    22→**Key Steps:** Detect tools → Determine tier → Write forge-tier.yaml → QMD hygiene (Deep)
    23→
    24→**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→### Brief Skill (BS)
    29→
    30→**Command:** `@Ferris BS`
    31→
    32→**Purpose:** Scope and design a skill through guided discovery.
    33→
    34→**When to Use:** Before `Create Skill` when you want maximum control over what gets compiled.
    35→
    36→**Key Steps:** Gather intent → Analyze target → Define scope → Write skill-brief.yaml
    37→
    38→**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→### Create Skill (CS)
    43→
    44→**Command:** `@Ferris CS`
    45→
    46→**Purpose:** Compile a skill from a brief. Supports `--batch` for multiple briefs.
    47→
    48→**When to Use:** After Brief Skill, or with an existing skill-brief.yaml.
    49→
    50→**Key Steps:** Load brief → Ecosystem check → AST extract → QMD enrich → Compile → Validate → Generate
    51→
    52→**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)
    53→
    54→---
    55→
    56→### Update Skill (US)
    57→
    58→**Command:** `@Ferris US`
    59→
    60→**Purpose:** Smart regeneration preserving `[MANUAL]` sections. Detects individual vs stack internally.
    61→
    62→**When to Use:** After source code changes when an existing skill needs updating.
    63→
    64→**Key Steps:** Load existing → Detect changes → Re-extract → Merge (preserve MANUAL) → Validate → Write
    65→
    66→**Agent:** Ferris (Surgeon mode)
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Feature Workflows
    71→
    72→### Quick Skill (QS)
    73→
    74→**Command:** `@Ferris QS <package-or-url>`
    75→
    76→**Purpose:** Brief-less fast skill with package-to-repo resolution.
    77→
    78→**When to Use:** When you need a skill quickly — no brief needed. Accepts package names or GitHub URLs.
    79→
    80→**Key Steps:** Resolve target → Ecosystem check → Quick extract → Compile → Validate → Write
    81→
    82→**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→### Stack Skill (SS)
    87→
    88→**Command:** `@Ferris SS`
    89→
    90→**Purpose:** Consolidated project stack skill with integration patterns.
    91→
    92→**When to Use:** When you want your agent to understand your entire project stack — not just individual libraries.
    93→
    94→**Key Steps:** Detect manifests → Rank dependencies → Scope confirmation → Parallel extract → Detect integrations → Compile stack → Generate references
    95→
    96→**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)
    97→
    98→---
    99→
   100→### Analyze Source (AN)
   101→
   102→**Command:** `@Ferris AN`
   103→
   104→**Purpose:** Decomposition engine — discover what to skill, recommend stack skill.
   105→
   106→**When to Use:** Brownfield onboarding of large repos or multi-service projects.
   107→
   108→**Key Steps:** Scan project → Identify units → Map exports → Detect integrations → Recommend → Generate briefs
   109→
   110→**Agent:** Ferris (Architect mode)
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→### Audit Skill (AS)
   115→
   116→**Command:** `@Ferris AS`
   117→
   118→**Purpose:** Drift detection between skill and current source.
   119→
   120→**When to Use:** To check if a skill has fallen out of date with its source code.
   121→
   122→**Key Steps:** Load skill → Re-index source → Structural diff → Semantic diff (Deep) → Classify severity → Report
   123→
   124→**Agent:** Ferris (Audit mode)
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→### Test Skill (TS)
   129→
   130→**Command:** `@Ferris TS`
   131→
   132→**Purpose:** Cognitive completeness verification. Quality gate before export.
   133→
   134→**When to Use:** After creating or updating a skill, before exporting.
   135→
   136→**Key Steps:** Load skill → Detect mode → Coverage check → Coherence check → Score → Gap report
   137→
   138→**Agent:** Ferris (Audit mode)
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→## Utility Workflows
   143→
   144→### Export Skill (EX)
   145→
   146→**Command:** `@Ferris EX`
   147→
   148→**Purpose:** Package for distribution + platform-aware context injection into CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md.
   149→
   150→**When to Use:** When a skill is ready for distribution or CLAUDE.md integration.
   151→
   152→**Key Steps:** Load skill → Package → Generate snippet → Update CLAUDE.md → Token report → Summary
   153→
   154→**Agent:** Ferris (Delivery mode)
   155→
   156→---
   157→
   158→## Workflow Connections
   159→
   160→```
   161→                    setup-forge (one-time)
   162→                         │
   163→          ┌──────────────┼──────────────┐
   164→          │              │              │
   165→     analyze-source  quick-skill  create-stack-skill
   166→          │              │              │
   167→     brief-skill         │              │
   168→          │              │              │
   169→     create-skill        │              │
   170→          │              │              │
   171→          └──────┬───────┘──────────────┘
   172→                 │
   173→            test-skill (quality gate)
   174→                 │
   175→          ┌──────┼──────┐
   176→          │             │
   177→     export-skill  audit-skill
   178→                        │
   179→                   update-skill (loops back to test)
   180→```
   181→
   182→---
   183→
   184→## Build Priority
   185→
   186→| Phase | Workflows | Notes |
   187→|-------|-----------|-------|
   188→| 1a (Core Engine) | setup-forge, create-skill, test-skill, export-skill | Must build first |
   189→| 1b (Entry Points) | quick-skill, brief-skill, create-stack-skill | Complete v1.0 |
   190→| 2 (Lifecycle) | audit-skill, update-skill, analyze-source | Post-launch |
   191→

     7→
     8→# Update Skill
     9→
    10→**Goal:** Surgically update existing skills when source code changes, preserving all [MANUAL] developer content while re-extracting only affected exports with full provenance tracking.
    11→
    12→**Your Role:** In addition to your name, communication_style, and persona, you are also a precision code analyst operating in Surgeon mode. This is a surgical operation, not an exploratory session. You bring AST-backed structural analysis and provenance-driven change detection expertise, while the source code provides the ground truth. Every regenerated instruction must trace to code with file:line citations.
    13→
    14→## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE
    15→
    16→### Core Principles
    17→
    18→- **Micro-file Design**: Each step is a self-contained instruction file that must be followed exactly
    19→- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only the current step file is in memory — never load future step files until told to do so
    20→- **Sequential Enforcement**: Sequence within the step files must be completed in order, no skipping or optimization allowed
    21→- **Surgical Precision**: Only changed exports are re-extracted — unchanged content is never touched
    22→- **[MANUAL] Preservation**: Developer-authored [MANUAL] sections survive regeneration with zero content loss
    23→- **Zero Hallucination**: Every instruction must trace to actual code with AST file:line citations and confidence tier labels
    24→
    25→### Step Processing Rules
    26→
    27→1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Always read the entire step file before taking any action
    28→2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute all numbered sections in order, never deviate
    29→3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: If a menu is presented, halt and wait for user selection
    30→4. **CHECK CONTINUATION**: If the step has a menu with Continue as an option, only proceed to next step when user selects 'C' (Continue)
    31→5. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, load, read entire file, then execute the next step file
    32→
    33→### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)
    34→
    35→- 🛑 **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
    36→- 📖 **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
    37→- 🚫 **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
    38→- 🎯 **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
    39→- ⏸️ **ALWAYS** halt at menus and wait for user input
    40→- 📋 **NEVER** create mental todo lists from future steps
    41→- 🔬 **NEVER** hallucinate — every statement must have AST provenance
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE
    46→
    47→### 1. Module Configuration Loading
    48→
    49→Load and read full config from {project-root}/_bmad/skf/config.yaml and resolve:
    50→
    51→- `project_name`, `output_folder`, `user_name`, `communication_language`, `document_output_language`
    52→- `skills_output_folder`, `forge_data_folder`, `sidecar_path`
    53→
    54→### 2. First Step Execution
    55→
    56→Load, read the full file and then execute ./steps-c/step-01-init.md to begin the workflow.
    57→

     7→
     8→# Step 3: Extract
     9→
    10→## STEP GOAL:
    11→
    12→To extract all public exports, function signatures, type definitions, and co-import patterns from the source code using tier-appropriate tools, building a complete extraction inventory with confidence-tiered provenance citations.
    13→
    14→## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    15→
    16→### Universal Rules:
    17→
    18→- 📖 CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    19→- 🎯 ALWAYS follow the exact instructions in the step file
    20→- ⚙️ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    21→- ✅ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    22→
    23→### Role Reinforcement:
    24→
    25→- ✅ You are a skill compilation engine performing structural extraction
    26→- ✅ Zero hallucination tolerance — every extracted item must trace to source code
    27→- ✅ Confidence tier labeling is mandatory for every extraction result
    28→
    29→### Step-Specific Rules:
    30→
    31→- 🎯 Focus ONLY on extracting exports, signatures, types from source code
    32→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to compile SKILL.md content — that's step-05
    33→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to write any output files — extraction stays in context
    34→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to include content that cannot be cited to a source location
    35→- ⚒️ Every extracted item MUST have a provenance citation: `[AST:{file}:L{line}]` or `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
    36→
    37→## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    38→
    39→- 🎯 Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    40→- 💾 Build extraction inventory in context — do not write files
    41→- 📖 Load extraction patterns data file for tier-specific strategy
    42→- 🚫 If an export cannot be verified, exclude it — do not guess
    43→
    44→## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    45→
    46→- Available: brief_data, tier, source_location, file_tree from step-01; ecosystem_status from step-02
    47→- Focus: Source code extraction and inventory building
    48→- Limits: Do NOT compile, assemble, or write any output
    49→- Dependencies: Source code must be accessible (resolved in step-01)
    50→
    51→## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    52→
    53→**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise.
    54→
    55→### 1. Load Extraction Patterns
    56→
    57→Load `{extractionPatternsData}` completely. Identify the strategy for the current forge tier.
    58→
    59→### 2. Apply Scope Filters
    60→
    61→From the brief, apply scope and pattern filters:
    62→
    63→- `scope` — determines what to extract (e.g., "all public exports", specific modules)
    64→- `include_patterns` — file globs to include (if specified)
    65→- `exclude_patterns` — file globs to exclude (if specified)
    66→
    67→Build the filtered file list from the source tree resolved in step-01.
    68→
    69→### 3. Check for Docs-Only Mode
    70→
    71→**If `source_type: "docs-only"` in the brief data:**
    72→
    73→"**Docs-only mode:** No source code to extract. Documentation content will be fetched from `doc_urls` in step-03c."
    74→
    75→Build an empty extraction inventory with zero exports. Set `extraction_mode: "docs-only"` in context. Auto-proceed through Gate 2 (section 5) — display the empty inventory and note that T3 content will be produced by the doc-fetcher step.
    76→
    77→**If `source_type: "source"` (default):** Continue with extraction below.
    78→
    79→### 4. Execute Tier-Dependent Extraction
    80→
    81→**Remote Source Resolution (Forge/Deep only):**
    82→
    83→If `source_repo` is a local path: proceed with the tier-appropriate strategy as normal.
    84→
    85→If `source_repo` is a remote URL (GitHub URL or owner/repo format) AND tier is Forge or Deep:
    86→
    87→1. **Check `git` availability:** Verify `git` is functional (`git --version`). If `git` is not available, skip to the fallback warning below.
    88→
    89→2. **Ephemeral shallow clone:** Clone the repository to a system temp path for AST access:
    90→
    91→   ```
    92→   temp_path = {system_temp}/skf-ephemeral-{skill-name}-{timestamp}/
    93→   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none {source_repo} {temp_path}
    94→   ```
    95→
    96→   If `include_patterns` are specified in the brief, use sparse-checkout to limit the clone scope:
    97→
    98→   ```
    99→   git clone --depth 1 --branch {branch} --single-branch --filter=blob:none --sparse {source_repo} {temp_path}
   100→   git -C {temp_path} sparse-checkout set {include_patterns}
   101→   ```
   102→
   103→3. **If clone succeeds:** Update the working source path to `{temp_path}` for all subsequent AST operations in this step. Proceed with the **Forge/Deep Tier** extraction strategy below. Mark `ephemeral_clone_active = true` for cleanup.
   104→
   105→4. **If clone fails (network error, auth failure, timeout):**
   106→
   107→   ⚠️ **Warn the user explicitly:**
   108→
   109→   "Ephemeral clone of `{source_repo}` failed: {error}. Degrading to source reading (T1-low) for this run. For T1 (AST-verified) confidence, clone the repository locally and update `source_repo` in your brief to the local path."
   110→
   111→   Proceed with Quick tier extraction strategy below. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   112→
   113→**Ephemeral clone cleanup:** After extraction is complete for all files in scope (whether successful or partially failed), before presenting the Gate 2 summary (Section 6), if `ephemeral_clone_active`, delete the `{temp_path}` directory. Log: "Ephemeral source clone cleaned up." This ensures cleanup runs even if some extractions failed, as long as the step itself is still executing.
   114→
   115→**Quick Tier (No AST tools):**
   116→
   117→1. Use `gh_bridge.list_tree(owner, repo, branch)` to map source structure (if remote)
   118→2. Identify entry points: index files, main exports, public modules
   119→3. Use `gh_bridge.read_file(owner, repo, path)` to read each entry point
   120→4. Extract from source text: exported function names, parameter lists, return types
   121→5. Infer types from JSDoc, docstrings, type annotations
   122→6. Confidence: All results T1-low — `[SRC:{file}:L{line}]`
   123→
   124→**Forge/Deep Tier (AST available):**
   125→
   126→⚠️ **CRITICAL:** Before executing AST extraction, load the **AST Extraction Protocol** section from `{extractionPatternsData}`. Follow the decision tree based on the file count from step-01's file tree. This determines whether to use the MCP tool, scoped YAML rules, or CLI streaming. Never use `ast-grep --json` (without `=stream`) — it loads the entire result set into memory and will fail on large codebases.
   127→
   128→1. Detect language from brief or file extensions
   129→2. Follow the AST Extraction Protocol decision tree from `{extractionPatternsData}`:
   130→   - ≤100 files: use `find_code()` MCP tool with `max_results` and `output_format="text"`
   131→   - ≤500 files: use `find_code_by_rule()` MCP tool with scoped YAML rules
   132→   - >500 files: use CLI `--json=stream` with line-by-line streaming Python
   133→3. For each export: extract function name, full signature, parameter types, return type, line number
   134→4. Use `ast_bridge.detect_co_imports(path, libraries[])` to find integration points
   135→5. Build extraction rules YAML data for reproducibility
   136→6. Confidence: All results T1 — `[AST:{file}:L{line}]`
   137→
   138→**If AST tool is unavailable at Forge/Deep tier:**
   139→
   140→⚠️ **Warn the user explicitly:** "AST tools are unavailable — extraction will use source reading (T1-low). Run [SF] Setup Forge to detect and configure AST tools for T1 confidence."
   141→
   142→Degrade to Quick tier extraction. Note the degradation reason in context for the evidence report.
   143→
   144→**For each file — handle failures gracefully:**
   145→
   146→- If a file cannot be read: log warning, skip file, continue with remaining files
   147→- If AST parsing fails on a file: fall back to source reading for that file, continue
   148→
   149→### 5. Build Extraction Inventory
   150→
   151→Compile all extracted data into a structured inventory:
   152→
   153→**Per-export entry:**
   154→- Function/type name
   155→- Full signature with types
   156→- Parameters (name, type, required/optional)
   157→- Return type
   158→- Source file and line number
   159→- Provenance citation (`[AST:...]` or `[SRC:...]`)
   160→- Confidence tier (T1 or T1-low)
   161→
   162→**Aggregate counts:**
   163→- Total files scanned
   164→- Total exports found
   165→- Exports by type (functions, types/interfaces, constants)
   166→- Confidence breakdown (T1 count, T1-low count)
   167→- `top_exports[]` — sorted list of the top 10-20 public API function names by prominence (import frequency or documentation position). This named field is consumed by step-03b for targeted temporal fetching and cache fingerprinting.
   168→
   169→**Co-import patterns (Forge/Deep only):**
   170→- Libraries commonly imported alongside extracted exports
   171→- Integration point suggestions
   172→
   173→### 6. Present Extraction Summary (Gate 2)
   174→
   175→Display the extraction findings for user confirmation:
   176→
   177→"**Extraction complete.**
   178→
   179→**Files scanned:** {file_count}
   180→**Exports found:** {export_count} ({function_count} functions, {type_count} types, {constant_count} constants)
   181→**Confidence:** {t1_count} T1 (AST-verified), {t1_low_count} T1-low (source reading)
   182→**Tier used:** {tier}
   183→**Co-import patterns:** {pattern_count} detected
   184→
   185→**Top exports:**
   186→{list top 10 exports with signatures}
   187→
   188→{warnings if any files skipped or degraded}
   189→
   190→Review the extraction summary above. Select an option to continue."
   191→
   192→### 7. Present MENU OPTIONS
   193→
   194→Display: "**Extraction Summary — Select an Option:** [C] Continue to compilation"
   195→
   196→#### EXECUTION RULES:
   197→
   198→- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting the extraction summary
   199→- This is Gate 2 — user must confirm before compilation proceeds
   200→- User may ask questions about the extraction results before continuing
   201→
   202→#### Menu Handling Logic:
   203→
   204→- IF C: Confirm extraction inventory is complete. Immediately load, read entire file, then execute `{nextStepFile}`
   205→- IF Any other comments or queries: answer questions about the extraction results, then redisplay the menu
   206→
   207→## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   208→
   209→ONLY WHEN the extraction inventory is built with provenance citations and the user has confirmed the extraction summary will you proceed to load `{nextStepFile}` for temporal context fetching.
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## 🚨 SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   214→
   215→### ✅ SUCCESS:
   216→
   217→- Extraction patterns loaded and tier-appropriate strategy applied
   218→- Scope filters applied from brief
   219→- All accessible source files in scope scanned
   220→- Every extracted item has a provenance citation with confidence tier
   221→- Extraction inventory built with aggregate counts
   222→- Graceful degradation if AST tools unavailable
   223→- Gate 2 summary presented with export counts and confidence breakdown
   224→- User confirmed before proceeding
   225→
   226→### ❌ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   227→
   228→- Including exports without provenance citations
   229→- Guessing or hallucinating function signatures not in source
   230→- Not applying scope/include/exclude filters from the brief
   231→- Halting on individual file read failures instead of skipping
   232→- Not presenting Gate 2 summary for user confirmation
   233→- Beginning compilation or SKILL.md assembly in this step
   234→- Not reporting confidence tier breakdown
   235→
   236→**Master Rule:** Zero hallucination — every extraction must trace to source code. Uncitable content is excluded, not guessed.
   237→

     3→description: 'Load existing skill artifacts, provenance map, and forge tier; detect skill type; present baseline and change scope'

     7→---
     8→
     9→# Step 1: Initialize Update
    10→
    11→## STEP GOAL:
    12→
    13→Load the existing skill and all its provenance data, detect whether this is an individual or stack skill, load the forge tier configuration, and present a baseline summary so the user can confirm the update scope before proceeding.
    14→
    15→## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    16→
    17→### Universal Rules:
    18→
    19→- 🛑 NEVER generate content without user input
    20→- 📖 CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    21→- 🔄 CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    22→- 📋 YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    23→- ⚙️ TOOL/SUBPROCESS FALLBACK: If any instruction references a subprocess, subagent, or tool you do not have access to, you MUST still achieve the outcome in your main context thread
    24→- ✅ YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
    25→
    26→### Role Reinforcement:
    27→
    28→- ✅ You are a precision code analyst operating in Surgeon mode
    29→- ✅ Zero-hallucination principle: every statement must trace to actual artifacts
    30→- ✅ Clinical, terse communication — confidence-labeled, AST-backed
    31→- ✅ You bring provenance-driven analysis expertise; the source code provides ground truth
    32→
    33→### Step-Specific Rules:
    34→
    35→- 🎯 Focus ONLY on loading existing artifacts and establishing the baseline
    36→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to modify any files in this step — read-only operations
    37→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to begin change detection — that is step 02
    38→- 💬 Present findings with confidence tier labels on all loaded data
    39→
    40→## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    41→
    42→- 🎯 Follow MANDATORY SEQUENCE exactly
    43→- 💾 Load all required artifacts into working context
    44→- 📖 Validate artifact existence before proceeding
    45→- 🚫 FORBIDDEN to proceed without user confirmation at gate
    46→
    47→## CONTEXT BOUNDARIES:
    48→
    49→- Available: SKF module config (skills_output_folder, forge_data_folder), user-provided skill path
    50→- Focus: artifact loading and baseline establishment
    51→- Limits: read-only — do not modify any files
    52→- Dependencies: setup-forge must have been run (forge-tier.yaml), create-skill or create-stack-skill must have been run (SKILL.md + provenance-map.json)
    53→
    54→## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
    55→
    56→**CRITICAL:** Follow this sequence exactly. Do not skip, reorder, or improvise unless user explicitly requests a change.
    57→
    58→### 1. Request Skill Path
    59→
    60→"**Which skill would you like to update?**
    61→
    62→Provide either:
    63→- A skill name (resolves to `{skills_output_folder}/{name}/`)
    64→- A full path to the skill folder
    65→
    66→**Skill:** {user provides path or name}"
    67→
    68→Resolve the path to an absolute skill folder location.
    69→
    70→### 2. Validate Required Artifacts
    71→
    72→**Check SKILL.md exists:**
    73→- Load `{resolved_skill_path}/SKILL.md`
    74→- If missing: **ABORT** — "No SKILL.md found at `{resolved_skill_path}`. Run create-skill first."
    75→
    76→**Check metadata.json exists:**
    77→- Load `{resolved_skill_path}/metadata.json`
    78→- Extract: `name`, `skill_type` (single or stack), `version`, `generation_date`, `confidence_tier`, `source_root`
    79→- If missing: **ABORT** — "No metadata.json found. This skill may have been created manually. Run create-skill to generate provenance data."
    80→
    81→**Detect skill type from metadata:**
    82→- If `skill_type == "stack"`: flag as stack skill (multi-file update mode)
    83→- If `skill_type == "single"` or absent: flag as single skill
    84→
    85→### 3. Load Forge Tier Configuration
    86→
    87→**Load `{sidecar_path}/forge-tier.yaml`:**
    88→- Extract: `tier` (Quick, Forge, or Deep), available tools
    89→- If missing: **ABORT** — "No forge-tier.yaml found. Run setup-forge first to detect available tools."
    90→
    91→**Apply tier override:** Read `{sidecar_path}/preferences.yaml`. If `tier_override` is set and is a valid tier value (Quick, Forge, or Deep), use it instead of the detected tier.
    92→
    93→**Determine analysis capabilities:**
    94→- **Quick:** text pattern matching only → T1-low confidence
    95→- **Forge:** AST structural extraction → T1 confidence
    96→- **Deep:** AST + QMD semantic enrichment → T1 + T2 confidence
    97→
    98→### 4. Load Provenance Map
    99→
   100→**Load `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/provenance-map.json`:**
   101→- Extract: export list, file mappings, extraction timestamps, confidence tiers
   102→- Calculate provenance age (days since last extraction)
   103→
   104→**If provenance map missing:**
   105→
   106→"**WARNING:** No provenance map found at `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/provenance-map.json`.
   107→
   108→Without a provenance map, update-skill cannot perform targeted change detection. Options:
   109→
   110→**[D]egraded mode** — Perform full re-extraction with T1-low confidence (equivalent to re-running create-skill but preserving [MANUAL] sections)
   111→**[X]** — Abort and run create-skill first to generate provenance data
   112→
   113→Select: [D] Degraded / [X] Abort"
   114→
   115→- If D: set `degraded_mode = true`, proceed with full extraction scope
   116→- If X: **ABORT**
   117→
   118→### 5. Load [MANUAL] Section Inventory
   119→
   120→Load {manualSectionRulesFile} to understand [MANUAL] detection patterns.
   121→
   122→**Scan SKILL.md for [MANUAL] sections:**
   123→- Count all `<!-- [MANUAL:*] -->` markers
   124→- Map each [MANUAL] block to its parent section (by heading hierarchy)
   125→- Record section names and approximate line positions
   126→
   127→**For stack skills, also scan:**
   128→- All `references/*.md` files for [MANUAL] markers
   129→- All `references/integrations/*.md` files for [MANUAL] markers
   130→
   131→### 6. Resolve Source Code Path
   132→
   133→**From provenance map (if available):**
   134→- Extract `source_root` path
   135→- Validate source path exists and is accessible
   136→
   137→**If source path invalid or missing:**
   138→
   139→"**Source path from provenance map is invalid:** `{source_root}`
   140→
   141→Please provide the current source code path:
   142→**Path:** {user provides path}"
   143→
   144→### 7. Present Baseline Summary
   145→
   146→"**Update Skill Baseline:**
   147→
   148→| Property | Value |
   149→|----------|-------|
   150→| **Skill** | {skill_name} |
   151→| **Type** | {single/stack} |
   152→| **Version** | {version} |
   153→| **Created** | {created date} |
   154→| **Source** | {source_root} |
   155→| **Forge Tier** | {forge_tier} (current) vs {original_tier} (at creation) |
   156→| **Provenance Age** | {days} days since last extraction |
   157→| **Exports** | {export_count} tracked exports |
   158→| **[MANUAL] Sections** | {manual_count} preserved sections |
   159→| **Mode** | {normal/degraded} |
   160→
   161→**Analysis plan:** {tier_description}
   162→- {Quick: text pattern diff → T1-low findings}
   163→- {Forge: AST structural diff → T1 findings}
   164→- {Deep: AST structural + QMD semantic diff → T1 + T2 findings}
   165→
   166→**Ready to detect changes and update this skill?**"
   167→
   168→### 8. Present MENU OPTIONS
   169→
   170→Display: "**Select:** [C] Continue to Change Detection"
   171→
   172→#### Menu Handling Logic:
   173→
   174→- IF C: Load, read entire file, then execute {nextStepFile}
   175→- IF Any other: help user respond, then [Redisplay Menu Options](#8-present-menu-options)
   176→
   177→#### EXECUTION RULES:
   178→
   179→- ALWAYS halt and wait for user input after presenting menu
   180→- ONLY proceed to next step when user selects 'C'
   181→
   182→## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
   183→
   184→ONLY WHEN [C] is selected and baseline has been established with all required artifacts loaded, will you then load and read fully `{nextStepFile}` to execute change detection.
   185→
   186→---
   187→
   188→## 🚨 SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS
   189→
   190→### ✅ SUCCESS:
   191→
   192→- SKILL.md loaded and validated
   193→- metadata.json loaded with name, skill_type, version, source_root
   194→- Forge tier loaded from sidecar/forge-tier.yaml
   195→- Provenance map loaded (or degraded mode confirmed)
   196→- [MANUAL] sections inventoried across all output files
   197→- Source code path validated
   198→- Baseline summary presented to user
   199→- User confirms scope via [C] Continue
   200→
   201→### ❌ SYSTEM FAILURE:
   202→
   203→- Not validating artifact existence before proceeding
   204→- Not detecting skill type (individual vs stack)
   205→- Modifying any files during this read-only step
   206→- Proceeding without user confirmation
   207→- Not inventorying [MANUAL] sections before update begins
   208→- Not loading forge tier configuration
   209→
   210→**Master Rule:** Skipping steps, optimizing sequences, or not following exact instructions is FORBIDDEN and constitutes SYSTEM FAILURE.
   211→

1→# Context Snippet Format (ADR-L v2)
     2→
     3→## Format Rules
     4→
     5→- Consistent multi-line format per skill — no adaptive switching
     6→- ~50-80 tokens per skill target (description + exports + optional refs)
     7→- T1-now content only (AST-current, no annotations)
     8→- Description line is the WHEN-TO-USE signal — compressed from SKILL.md frontmatter
     9→- Refs line is the WHERE-TO-LOOK signal — only when references/ exists
    10→
    11→## Single Skill Snippet Template
    12→
    13→```markdown
    14→{skill-name} → skills/{skill-name}/
    15→  {compressed-description — ~15 words from SKILL.md frontmatter description}
    16→  exports: {export-1}, {export-2}, {export-3}, {export-4}, {export-5}
    17→  refs: {ref-1}, {ref-2}, {ref-3}
    18→```
    19→
    20→- **Line 1:** Name + path pointer (always present)
    21→- **Line 2:** Compressed description from SKILL.md frontmatter `description:` field (~15 words, trigger-optimized)
    22→- **Line 3:** Top exports from metadata.json `exports` array (up to 10 for Deep tier, 5 otherwise)
    23→- **Line 4:** Reference file names without extensions (only when `references/` directory exists and contains files)
    24→
    25→## Stack Skill Snippet Template
    26→
    27→```markdown
    28→{project}-stack → skills/{project}-stack/
    29→  {compressed-description}
    30→  stack: {dep-1}@{v1}, {dep-2}@{v2}, {dep-3}@{v3}
    31→  integrations: {pattern-1}, {pattern-2}, {pattern-3}
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Rules
    35→
    36→- **description**: Compress SKILL.md frontmatter `description:` to ~15 words. Focus on WHAT it does and WHEN to use it.
    37→- **exports**: Top exports from metadata.json `exports` array (by order as listed)
    38→- **refs**: File names from `references/` directory, without `.md` extension, sorted. Omit line if no references/ exists.
    39→- **stack**: Component versions from metadata.json `components` for stack skills
    40→- **integrations**: Co-import patterns from metadata.json `integrations` for stack skills
    41→- If fewer exports than the limit, list all available
    42→- If no exports data available, omit the exports line
    43→- Skill path is relative to project root
    44→

80→
    81→---
    82→
    83→## context-snippet.md Format (ADR-L v2)
    84→
    85→Compressed multi-line-per-skill format for CLAUDE.md managed section (~50-80 tokens per skill):
    86→
    87→```markdown
    88→{skill-name} → skills/{skill-name}/
    89→  {compressed description ~15 words from SKILL.md frontmatter}
    90→  exports: {comma-separated top 10 function names}
    91→  refs: {reference-file-names without .md extension}
    92→```
    93→
    94→- Line 2 (description): Compressed from SKILL.md frontmatter `description:` — the WHEN-TO-USE signal
    95→- Line 3 (exports): Top exports from metadata.json
    96→- Line 4 (refs): Only present when `references/` directory exists — the WHERE-TO-LOOK signal
    97→
    98→---
    99→
   100→## metadata.json Structure
   101→
   102→```json
   103→{
   104→  "name": "{skill-name}",
   105→  "version": "{source-version}",
   106→  "skill_type": "single",
   107→  "source_authority": "{official|community|internal}",
   108→  "source_repo": "{github-url}",
   109→  "source_root": "{resolved-source-path}",
   110→  "source_commit": "{commit-hash}",
   111→  "confidence_tier": "{Quick|Forge|Deep}",
   112→  "spec_version": "1.3",
   113→  "generation_date": "{ISO-8601}",
   114→  "exports": [],
   115→  "tool_versions": {
   116→    "ast_grep": "{version-or-null}",
   117→    "qmd": "{version-or-null}",
   118→    "skf": "1.0.0"
   119→  },
   120→  "stats": {
   121→    "exports_documented": 0,
   122→    "exports_total": 0,
   123→    "coverage": 0.0,
   124→    "confidence_t1": 0,
   125→    "confidence_t2": 0,
   126→    "confidence_t3": 0
   127→  },
   128→  "dependencies": [],
   129→  "compatibility": "{semver-range}"
